includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Contamination Removal Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive database of contamination removal parameters, safety data, and laser cleaning specifications

contaminant.name: Mold and Mildew Growth
contaminant.description: After dampness occurs, mold-mildew forms from fungal colonization on surfaces and releases organic acids with pigmented spores. Unique traits include spore pigmentation so it differs from rust or dust buildup. On woods like oak and drywall, penetration happens deeply while on concrete it stays surface-level, causing challenges in removal because residues cling tightly. Laser cleaning succeeds so because ablation targets organics precisely without harming substrates.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Carbon Monoxide and Mycotoxins generation - multiple toxic compounds
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: Half-face or full-face respirator with organic vapor/particulate cartridges, adequate ventilation

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (12 ACH) for particulate control
